title: [PROJECT] Image Optimization & Performance
description: Convert gallery images to WebP format and implement Next.js Image component with modern loading strategies to reduce page weight from ~70MB to <10MB and improve LCP scores

title: Convert 14 gallery PNG images to WebP format with automated quality optimization and size validation
Migrate all gallery tags to Next.js Image component with responsive srcSet and lazy loading
description:
FILES TO MODIFY: - `website/src/app/gallery/page.tsx` - Main gallery grid (primary modification) - `website/next.config.js` or `website/next.config.ts` - Image optimization config - Any modal/lightbox components (search codebase for `]*src[^>]*gallery` pattern) IMPLEMENTATION: 1. Update Next.js config: ```typescript // website/next.config.ts const config = { images: { formats: ['image/webp'], deviceSizes: [640, 750, 828, 1080, 1200, 1920], imageSizes: [16, 32, 48, 64, 96, 128, 256, 384], minimumCacheTTL: 31536000, // 1 year for static gallery }, }; ``` 2. Gallery grid pattern (assuming equal aspect ratios): ```tsx import Image from 'next/image'; // For fixed-size grid items: // OR for responsive containers with varying aspect ratios:
``` 3. Handle first image above-the-fold: ```tsx ``` 4. Verify all instances replaced: `grep -rn "` tags in gallery-related files: `grep -r "]*gallery" website/src/` returns nothing - No Next.js Image optimization warnings in `npm run build` output - DevTools Network tab shows multiple image sizes loaded (e.g., `gallery/image.webp?w=640`, `?w=828`, `?w=1080`) based on viewport - No layout shift: run Lighthouse CLS score <0.1 - Lighthouse Performance score increases 15+ points (baseline vs optimized) - LCP <2.5s on "Fast 3G" throttling (Chrome DevTools) - Gallery page hydration time <500ms (measure with React DevTools Profiler)

title: Generate and integrate base64 blur placeholder data URLs for all 14 gallery images with automated data.ts update
[PROJECT] SEO & Meta Tag Implementation
description: Implement comprehensive SEO foundation with Open Graph, Twitter Cards, structured data, and sitemap to improve social sharing and search visibility

title: Implement Next.js 14 Metadata API with Open Graph and Twitter Cards
description:
FILES TO MODIFY:
1. website/src/app/layout.tsx - Add default metadata export:
   - Import type { Metadata } from 'next'
   - Export const metadata: Metadata = { metadataBase: new URL('https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app'), title: { template: '%s | Pawcasso Atelier', default: 'Pawcasso Atelier - Custom AI Pet Portraits' }, description: 'Transform your pet into stunning AI-generated art. Custom portraits for $9.', openGraph: { type: 'website', locale: 'en_US', url: 'https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app', siteName: 'Pawcasso Atelier', images: [{ url: '/gallery/golden-retriever-renaissance.png', width: 1200, height: 630, alt: 'AI-generated golden retriever portrait' }] }, twitter: { card: 'summary_large_image', creator: '@pawcasso_atelier' } }
2. website/src/app/gallery/page.tsx - Add page-specific metadata export:
   - Import type { Metadata } from 'next' and galleryImages from '@/lib/data'
   - Export const metadata: Metadata = { title: 'Gallery', description: 'Browse our collection of AI-generated pet portraits featuring dogs, cats, and more in artistic styles', openGraph: { images: galleryImages.slice(0, 6).map(img => ({ url: img.src, alt: img.title })) } }
3. website/src/app/order/page.tsx - Add order page metadata:
   - Import type { Metadata } from 'next'
   - Export const metadata: Metadata = { title: 'Order Custom Portrait', description: 'Order your custom AI-generated pet portrait for $9. Fast delivery, stunning results.', openGraph: { type: 'website' } }

DEPENDENCIES: None (Metadata API built into Next.js 14)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Run 'curl -I https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app' shows og:title, og:description, og:image, twitter:card headers
- Validate with https://metatags.io/ - all OG and Twitter tags present
- Validate with https://cards-dev.twitter.com/validator - preview renders correctly
- Each page (/, /gallery, /order) has unique title and description

title: Add Schema.org JSON-LD for Product and ImageGallery
description:
FILES TO MODIFY:
1. website/src/app/order/page.tsx - Add Product schema:
   - After opening <div className="container...">, insert:
   <script
     type="application/ld+json"
     dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{
       __html: JSON.stringify({
         '@context': 'https://schema.org',
         '@type': 'Product',
         name: 'Custom AI Pet Portrait',
         image: 'https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app/gallery/golden-retriever-renaissance.png',
         description: 'Transform your pet into stunning AI-generated artwork. Custom digital portrait delivered within 48 hours.',
         brand: { '@type': 'Brand', name: 'Pawcasso Atelier' },
         offers: {
           '@type': 'Offer',
           url: 'https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app/order',
           priceCurrency: 'USD',
           price: '9.00',
           availability: 'https://schema.org/InStock',
           priceValidUntil: '2027-12-31'
         }
       })
     }}
   />
2. website/src/app/gallery/page.tsx - Add ImageGallery schema:
   - Import galleryImages from '@/lib/data'
   - After opening <div className="container...">, insert:
   <script
     type="application/ld+json"
     dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{
       __html: JSON.stringify({
         '@context': 'https://schema.org',
         '@type': 'ImageGallery',
         name: 'Pawcasso Atelier Gallery',
         description: 'Collection of AI-generated pet portraits in various artistic styles',
         image: galleryImages.map(img => ({
           '@type': 'ImageObject',
           contentUrl: `https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app${img.src}`,
           name: img.title,
           description: img.caption || img.title,
           encodingFormat: 'image/png'
         }))
       })
     }}
   />

DEPENDENCIES: None

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- View page source at /order - JSON-LD Product schema present with correct price, availability
- View page source at /gallery - JSON-LD ImageGallery schema present with all gallery images
- Validate with https://validator.schema.org/ - zero errors
- Test with https://search.google.com/test/rich-results - Product and ImageGallery recognized
- Run: curl https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app/order | grep 'application/ld+json' - returns Product schema
- Run: curl https://pawcasso-atelier.vercel.app/gallery | grep 'application/ld+json' - returns ImageGallery schema

title: Generate sitemap.xml and robots.txt with Next.js MetadataRoute API

description: Enable customers to upload a photo of their pet during order submission, storing it securely with Vercel Blob for the artist to reference

Add pet photo upload UI to order form with Stripe metadata persistence
[PROJECT] Mobile UX & Visual Polish
description: Enhance mobile experience and perceived performance with loading states, responsive improvements, and smooth transitions

title: Implement Gallery Skeleton Loading with React Suspense & Shimmer Animation
Optimize Gallery Filters & Order Form for Mobile Touch Interaction (320px-428px)
description:
FILES TO MODIFY: - website/src/app/gallery/page.tsx - website/src/app/order/page.tsx - website/src/app/globals.css DEPENDENCIES: None (pure CSS/Tailwind solution) IMPLEMENTATION: 1. MODIFY website/src/app/gallery/page.tsx (filter buttons): Find the filter buttons container (likely a `
` wrapping buttons) and update: ```tsx
{/* repeat for other filter buttons */}
``` 2. MODIFY website/src/app/order/page.tsx (form inputs): Update all form field elements: - Text inputs: `className="w-full min-h-[44px] px-4 text-base rounded-lg border ..."` - Textarea: `className="w-full min-h-[44px] px-4 py-3 text-base rounded-lg border ..." rows={4}` - Select: `className="w-full min-h-[44px] px-4 text-base rounded-lg border ..."` - Labels: `className="block mb-2 text-sm font-medium text-gray-700"` - Form container: add `className="space-y-4"` (or `space-y-6` if more breathing room desired) - Submit button: `className="min-h-[48px] w-full sm:w-auto px-8 text-base font-semibold ..."` 3. ADD TO website/src/app/globals.css (after @tailwind directives): ```css @layer utilities { .scrollbar-hide::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; } .scrollbar-hide { -ms-overflow-style: none; scrollbar-width: none; } } ``` ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: - [ ] Open Chrome DevTools responsive mode, test widths: 320px, 375px, 390px, 428px - [ ] Gallery page: Filter buttons scroll horizontally with touch/swipe, snap cleanly to each button - [ ] Gallery page: No horizontal scrollbar visible on desktop (scrollbar-hide working) - [ ] Order page: All input fields are 44px tall minimum (iOS/Android touch target spec) - [ ] Order page: No horizontal overflow at any tested width (run `document.body.scrollWidth === window.innerWidth` in console) - [ ] Order page: Text inputs use 16px font size (prevents iOS auto-zoom) - [ ] Order page: Submit button is full-width on mobile, auto-width on desktop (sm: breakpoint works) - [ ] Run Lighthouse mobile audit: Accessibility score ≥95, no touch target size errors - [ ] Test on real iOS/Android device: no awkward zoom-in when tapping inputs, smooth horizontal scroll on filters

title: [PROJECT] Premium Pricing & Upsells
description: Transform from single $9 product to tiered revenue model. Add Premium ($29), Deluxe ($49), and Bundle ($79) tiers with differentiated value. Increases average order value 3-5x and targets $1M revenue with 60% fewer customers.

title: Implement tiered product selection on order page
description: MODIFY website/src/app/order/page.tsx: Add pricing tier radio buttons before form. Tiers: Basic ($9, 1 portrait, 24hr delivery), Premium ($29, 1 portrait + 2 variations, 12hr delivery, high-res download), Deluxe ($49, 3 portraits, 6hr delivery, print-ready files), Bundle ($79, 5 portraits, instant delivery, commercial license). Each tier shows feature list and price. Store selected tier in state, pass to Stripe metadata. UPDATE website/src/lib/stripe.ts or create if missing: Map tier selection to corresponding Stripe Price IDs (create 4 products in Stripe dashboard first). ACCEPTANCE: Clicking each tier highlights selection, submit passes correct price to Stripe, Stripe dashboard shows tier in metadata, payment amount matches tier price.

title: Add post-purchase upsell modal for digital downloads
description: CREATE website/src/app/order/success/page.tsx (Stripe redirects here after payment): Show order confirmation, then modal offering Print Package ($19, high-res TIFF + PNG, print dimensions guide) and Commercial License ($99, usage rights document). Use state for modal visibility, fetch original order details from Stripe session_id URL param. CREATE website/src/app/api/upsell/route.ts: POST handler that creates new Stripe checkout session for upsell products, links to original order via metadata. MODIFY website/src/app/api/create-checkout-session/route.ts: Add success_url with session_id: `success_url: ${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_URL}/order/success?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}`. ACCEPTANCE: After payment, success page loads with 2-second delay before modal appears, clicking upsell redirects to Stripe checkout, declining closes modal and shows order details, upsell purchases link to original order in Stripe metadata.

title: Create comparison table showcasing tier value
description: CREATE website/src/components/PricingComparison.tsx: Responsive table component with 4 columns (Basic, Premium, Deluxe, Bundle). Rows: Number of portraits, Delivery time, Resolution, File formats, Revisions included, Commercial use, Print-ready files. Use checkmarks (✓) and X marks (✗) for features. Highlight 'Most Popular' badge on Premium tier. MODIFY website/src/app/order/page.tsx: Import and render <PricingComparison /> above order form. ADD TO website/src/app/page.tsx (homepage): Add pricing preview section with CTA linking to /order. STYLING: Use Tailwind with sticky header row on mobile, horizontal scroll for narrow viewports, accent color for Premium column border. ACCEPTANCE: Table renders on order page and homepage, mobile viewports scroll horizontally smoothly, Premium tier visually stands out, all features accurately reflect tier differences.

title: [PROJECT] Conversion Optimization & Social Proof
description: Install analytics and conversion tracking, implement social proof elements (testimonials, order counter), and add email capture to build marketing list. Goal: increase 2-5% baseline conversion rate to 8-12% through trust signals and retargeting.

title: Integrate Google Analytics 4 and Meta Pixel with event tracking
description: CREATE website/src/lib/analytics.ts: Export gtag() helper and trackEvent() function. MODIFY website/src/app/layout.tsx: Add GA4 and Meta Pixel scripts to <head> using next/script with strategy='afterInteractive'. Use env vars NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_ID and NEXT_PUBLIC_META_PIXEL_ID. MODIFY website/src/app/order/page.tsx: Track 'begin_checkout' event when form opens, 'add_payment_info' when Stripe redirect happens. MODIFY website/src/app/order/success/page.tsx: Track 'purchase' event with order value and tier from URL params. MODIFY website/src/app/gallery/page.tsx: Track 'view_gallery' event on mount. ADD TO website/.env.local: GA_ID and PIXEL_ID placeholders. ACCEPTANCE: Real-time GA4 dashboard shows events firing, Meta Events Manager shows pixel active, purchase events include revenue and tier metadata, no console errors, events fire on both dev and production.

title: Add testimonials carousel and live order counter to homepage
description: CREATE website/src/components/Testimonials.tsx: Carousel component with 6-8 customer testimonials (Name, Pet name, Photo, 5-star rating, Quote). Use Embla Carousel or react-slick for auto-scroll. CREATE website/src/components/LiveOrderCounter.tsx: Displays 'X orders in the last 24 hours' with incrementing animation. Use hardcoded seed number (e.g., 47) + Math.random() fluctuation, updates every 30 seconds. CREATE website/src/lib/testimonials.ts: Export testimonials array with mock data (use realistic pet names and quotes). MODIFY website/src/app/page.tsx: Add <Testimonials /> section after hero, add <LiveOrderCounter /> as floating badge in bottom-right corner with z-50. STYLING: Testimonials use card grid on desktop, carousel on mobile. Counter badge has subtle pulse animation. ACCEPTANCE: Carousel auto-scrolls every 4 seconds, manual swipe works on mobile, counter increments realistically (40-60 range), testimonials have pet photos (use gallery images as placeholders), component doesn't cause layout shift.

title: Implement email capture popup with exit-intent and welcome discount
description: CREATE website/src/components/EmailCaptureModal.tsx: Modal that shows on exit-intent (mouse leaves viewport) or after 45 seconds on site. Form with email input, 'Get 15% Off Your First Portrait' headline, privacy note. On submit, POST to /api/subscribe. Use localStorage to prevent showing again for 7 days after submission or dismissal. CREATE website/src/app/api/subscribe/route.ts: POST handler that adds email to Mailchimp or ConvertKit list (use MAILCHIMP_API_KEY and LIST_ID env vars). Return discount code 'FIRST15' on success. MODIFY website/src/app/layout.tsx: Render <EmailCaptureModal /> at root level. DEPENDENCIES: npm install @mailchimp/mailchimp_marketing (or chosen email provider SDK). ACCEPTANCE: Modal appears on exit-intent or 45s timer (test with DevTools), email submission succeeds and adds to Mailchimp list, discount code shows in confirmation message, modal doesn't reappear after dismissal (test with localStorage inspection), mobile version is full-screen with bottom sheet UI.

title: [PROJECT] Marketing Automation & Referral System
description: Build email automation sequences for abandoned carts, post-purchase follow-up, and referral incentives. Add customer referral program to drive organic growth. Target 20-30% of revenue from referrals within 90 days.

title: Set up abandoned cart email automation workflow
description: CREATE website/src/app/api/cart-abandoned/route.ts: Webhook endpoint that receives Stripe 'checkout.session.expired' events. Extracts customer email, selected tier, and timestamp. Sends email via Resend or SendGrid with discount code (10% off, 48hr expiry) and direct checkout link. CREATE website/src/lib/email-templates/abandoned-cart.tsx: React Email template with pet-themed design, tier recap, discount CTA, and 'Complete Your Order' button. MODIFY website/src/app/api/create-checkout-session/route.ts: Add customer_email field to Stripe session creation for abandoned cart tracking. ADD TO website/.env.local: RESEND_API_KEY or SENDGRID_API_KEY, EMAIL_FROM address. DEPENDENCIES: npm install resend react-email. ACCEPTANCE: Creating checkout session without completing triggers webhook after 24hrs, email arrives with correct tier and discount code, clicking CTA link pre-fills checkout with original selections, discount code applies correctly, webhook handles errors gracefully (logs to console/Sentry).

title: Create post-purchase email sequence with referral program
description: CREATE website/src/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts: Stripe webhook handler for 'checkout.session.completed' event. Triggers 3-email sequence: (1) Order confirmation + delivery timeline (immediate), (2) Request photo review + referral link (24hrs post-delivery), (3) Upsell print services (7 days post-delivery). CREATE website/src/lib/email-templates/: confirmation.tsx, request-review.tsx, print-upsell.tsx. CREATE website/src/app/referral/[code]/page.tsx: Landing page that shows referee discount (20% off) and credits referrer ($5 account credit). Tracks referral code in cookies, applies discount at checkout. CREATE website/src/lib/referral.ts: Generate unique referral codes (e.g., PAWCASSO-JOHN123), store in database or Stripe metadata. DEPENDENCIES: npm install @stripe/stripe-js. ACCEPTANCE: Completing order triggers confirmation email within 1 minute, review request email arrives 24hrs after purchase (test with Stripe test mode timestamps), referral link format is /referral/[unique-code], clicking referral link shows discount banner and stores code in cookies, checkout applies 20% discount and credits referrer $5 in Stripe metadata.

title: Build customer portal for order history and referral dashboard
description: CREATE website/src/app/portal/page.tsx: Protected customer dashboard showing order history, referral stats (clicks, conversions, earnings), and unique referral link. Use Stripe Customer Portal for subscription management and receipts. CREATE website/src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts: NextAuth.js setup with magic link email authentication (no passwords). CREATE website/src/middleware.ts: Protect /portal route, redirect to login if unauthenticated. CREATE website/src/components/ReferralDashboard.tsx: Card showing referral link (copy to clipboard), total referrals, total earnings, pending payouts. MODIFY website/src/app/order/success/page.tsx: Show 'Create Account' CTA linking to portal signup. DEPENDENCIES: npm install next-auth nodemailer. STYLING: Use same design system as main site, mobile-responsive cards. ACCEPTANCE: Visiting /portal unauthenticated redirects to login, magic link email arrives and logs user in, dashboard shows all past orders with status, referral link copies to clipboard, referral stats update in real-time (poll API every 30s), Stripe Customer Portal link works for managing payment methods.

title: [PROJECT] AI Fulfillment Automation & Quality Control
description: Automate the portrait generation workflow from order to delivery. Build n8n integration that processes uploaded pet photos, generates portraits via Manus/Replicate, applies quality checks, and emails final files. Goal: zero-touch fulfillment for 95% of orders.

title: Build n8n workflow for automated portrait generation pipeline
description: CREATE n8n workflow (JSON export to website/automation/portrait-generation.json): (1) Webhook trigger listens for Stripe checkout.session.completed events, (2) Extract pet photo URL from metadata, (3) Download image from Vercel Blob, (4) Call Manus or Replicate API for portrait generation (use FLUX or Stable Diffusion model with animal portrait fine-tune), (5) Generate multiple variations based on tier (Basic=1, Premium=3, Deluxe=5), (6) Upload results to Vercel Blob, (7) Send delivery email with download links via Resend, (8) Update Stripe metadata with delivery_status='completed'. CREATE website/src/app/api/webhooks/generate/route.ts: Alternative Next.js API route implementation if n8n not available. DEPENDENCIES: Manus API key, Replicate API token, n8n self-hosted or cloud instance. ACCEPTANCE: Completing order triggers workflow within 2 minutes, generated portraits match uploaded pet's breed/color, delivery email contains high-res download links, Stripe metadata updates with completion timestamp, failed generations retry 3 times with exponential backoff, workflow handles missing pet photo gracefully (uses default stock image).

title: Implement AI quality scoring and human review queue
description: CREATE website/src/app/api/quality-check/route.ts: Receives generated portrait URLs, runs quality assessment via OpenAI Vision API or Anthropic Claude Vision. Checks: (1) Face visibility (eyes, nose clear), (2) No distortions or artifacts, (3) Color accuracy vs original photo, (4) Artistic quality score 1-10. Portraits scoring <7 go to human review queue. CREATE website/src/app/admin/review/page.tsx: Admin dashboard showing low-scoring portraits with side-by-side comparison (original photo vs generated), 'Approve' or 'Regenerate' buttons. Protected with admin password or NextAuth admin role. CREATE website/src/lib/quality-db.ts: Simple SQLite or Vercel Postgres table storing portrait_id, score, status (pending|approved|rejected), reviewer_notes. DEPENDENCIES: npm install openai @vercel/postgres. ACCEPTANCE: Generated portraits auto-score via Vision API, scores >7 auto-approve and send immediately, scores <7 appear in admin queue, admin can regenerate portraits with one click (triggers new Manus generation), approved portraits send delivery email, rejected portraits refund customer via Stripe API, quality scores visible in order metadata.
